# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official node runtime as base image 
FROM node:14-alpine@sha256   # replace 'latest' with your preferred tag or hash if you want a specific version of NodeJS in the Docker Image. e.g., use "9" for latest LTS (Node Version) and then specify it as `NODE_VERSION=<tag> sh build-image.sh`
# Set environment variables 
ENV NODE_ENV production   # set your preferred env variable here, like 'development', or any other value you want to have in the container's process environments (e.g., DEBUG)
# Install npm packages and create a node app directory inside Docker image   
WORKDIR /app 
COPY package*.json ./   # copy your dependencies into docker file, replace 'package.*'. with all necessary files for react application like index.js or server.js etc... (e.g., if you have multiple entry points in the app) and npm install them inside Docker image using `npm ci`
RUN npm i  # run your dependencies installation here by executing command 'sh build-image.sh' after copying all necessary files into docker file, replace it with actual commands to execute during container building process (e.g., if you have multiple entry points in the app) and install them inside Docker image using `npm ci`
# Copy your source code from local machine 
COPY . ./   # copy entire application directory including all files into docker file, replace '.' with actual path of react project on host (e.g., /home/user/projects/) in the container and npm run build inside Docker image using `npm ci` or if you have multiple entry points
# Install dependencies for production bundle 
RUN npm install --production   # replace '--production' with your preferred command to use when installing packages (e.g., yarn, pnpm) in the container environment and also specify it as a build step inside Dockerfile using `sh` or if you have multiple entry points here for different environments 
# Build production bundle of React app   # replace 'build' with your preferred command to use when building packages (e.g., yarn, pnpm) in the container environment and also specify it as a build step inside Dockerfile using `sh` or if you have multiple entry points here for different environments 
RUN npm run build --if-present   # replace '--if-present' with your preferred command to use when building packages (e.g., yarn, pnpm) in the container environment and also specify it as a build step inside Dockerfile using `sh` or if you have multiple entry points here for different environments 
# Serve production bundle of React app   # replace 'serve' with your preferred command to use when serving packages (e.g., yarn, pnpm) in the container environment and also specify it as a build step inside Dockerfile using `sh` or if you have multiple entry points here for different environments 
CMD ["node", "server"]   # replace '--if-present' with your preferred command to use when serving packages (e.g., yarn, pnpm) in the container environment and also specify it as a build step inside Dockerfile using `sh` or if you have multiple entry points here for different environments
